symptom: Android APK app update fails with 'App installation failed' notification on user devices
rootCause: Users have removed the app from their devices. When Intune tries to update the app, the update fails because the app is no longer installed.
solution: Have the users reinstall the app from the Company Portal to their devices.

symptom: Applications don't appear as Available in the Intune Company Portal app on Windows 10 devices
rootCause: Users haven't identified their device in the Information Worker portal (IW portal) at https://portal.manage.microsoft.com.
solution: Users must go to portal.manage.microsoft.com → My Devices → Tap here → select their device to identify it. After identification, apps will appear on the Apps screen.

symptom: Cannot save corporate data in WIP-allowed apps (Word, Excel) on Hybrid Azure AD joined or co-managed devices. Error: 'Word cannot complete the save due to a file permission error' or Error 0x8007177C about invalid recovery certificate.
rootCause: The Encrypting File System (EFS) Data Recovery Agent (DRA) certificate in the organization's Group Policy has expired, preventing file encryption.
solution: 1) Export old DRA certificate and private key for decrypting existing files. 2) Create and verify a new EFS DRA certificate. 3) Update the Group Policy to use the new DRA certificate.

symptom: macOS devices are unexpectedly unenrolled from Microsoft Intune service or enrollment fails
rootCause: MDM agent mishandles failed MDM certificate installations. When the MDM agent doesn't receive expected headers, the client automatically removes its MDM enrollment profile.
solution: Re-enroll the affected macOS device.

symptom: Samsung Android Enterprise fully managed devices running Android 11+ show as noncompliant after a managed update is applied, potentially affecting access to corporate resources via Conditional Access
rootCause: Known Samsung issue with compliance state after managed update on fully managed devices.
solution: For DA and work profile: upgrade Company Portal to version 5.0.5358.0 (fix released Dec 2021). For fully managed: issue still active, monitor Intune Customer Success blog for updates.

symptom: Approximately 1% of iOS 13+ devices enrolled in Intune cannot perform password reset - the passcode reset token is not returned
rootCause: Apple bug in iOS 13 where the token needed to allow password reset is not returned for some enrolled devices. Fixed in iOS 13.3.1, but already-enrolled devices need re-enrollment.
solution: 1) Update device to iOS 13.3.1 or later. 2) Remove and re-enroll the device in Intune. Use PowerShell script to identify affected devices (see Intune Customer Success blog).

symptom: Profile error 'Invalid Profile: The configuration for your iPad/iPhone could not be downloaded' when enrolling iOS devices with Apple Configurator for Setup Assistant enrollment
rootCause: Invalid enrollment URL used in Apple Configurator setup.
solution: See workaround in Intune Customer Success blog post for correcting the enrollment URL.

symptom: Users are signed out of ALL Office mobile apps on iOS once they sign out of a single Office app (or auto-signed out), forced to reauthenticate, sometimes leading to authentication loop
rootCause: Known issue with app protection policies (MAM) on iOS where signing out of one Office app triggers sign-out from all managed Office apps.
solution: See workaround in Intune Customer Success blog post.

symptom: Samsung work profile devices missing certificates after updating to Android 12, affecting Gmail and AnyConnect VPN access
rootCause: Samsung Android 12 update issue causing certificate loss on work profile enrolled devices.
solution: See temporary workarounds in Intune Customer Success blog. Issue has been resolved.

symptom: 'Rename device' setting disabled for Windows devices that are Hybrid Azure AD joined in Intune admin center
rootCause: Renaming Hybrid Azure AD joined devices can cause device single sign-on errors after user password change. Microsoft intentionally disabled this feature.
solution: This is by design. Device renaming is only available for co-managed devices that are Azure AD joined (not hybrid joined).

symptom: Cannot uninstall iOS apps installed from Apple App Store using Intune - only MDM-deployed apps can be removed
rootCause: By design: Intune can only uninstall apps deployed through the MDM channel. Apps installed directly from App Store are not MDM-managed.
solution: 1) Add the apps to Intune and assign as Available or Required (prompts user to allow Intune management). 2) Change assignment type to Uninstall. For DEP supervised devices, management takeover prompt is skipped.

symptom: OneDrive configured as managed app with APP policy allows users to attach corporate protected files via iMessage using Open-In feature
rootCause: The Open-In feature is controlled by the OS, not by Intune APP. App protection policies cannot prevent this behavior.
solution: Use MDM General Intune iOS Configuration policy: set 'Allow managed documents in other unmanaged apps' and 'Allow unmanaged documents in other managed apps' to No.

symptom: 'Mismatch between the value type and the configuration value' error when iOS app configuration policy contains URL with ampersand (&) character
rootCause: iOS configuration profiles don't support the ampersand character in XML configuration files.
solution: Replace '&' with '&' in the URL, or create a short URL redirect that avoids the ampersand.

symptom: Intune APP policy 'Select which storage services corporate data can be saved to' does not prevent saving images from email messages locally in Outlook for iOS
rootCause: By design: the data relocation storage restriction setting does not apply to images in email messages in Outlook for iOS.
solution: No workaround available. This is by design limitation of current APP policy for Outlook iOS images.

symptom: macOS LOB app (.pkg) not installed on targeted devices with no error messages shown in Intune
rootCause: The .pkg package is missing required information: package version/CFBundleVersion strings and correct install-location (/Applications or subfolder).
solution: Contact app developer to rebuild .pkg package with correct pkg-info containing CFBundleVersion and install-location set to /Applications. Verify with: xar -x -f <pkg> -C <output> and check output for required fields.

symptom: Error 0x87D13BA2 'One or more apps contain invalid bundleIDs' when deploying macOS LOB app, even if app is successfully installed
rootCause: Multiple applications included in the macOS app package; not all individual applications report installation status back to Intune.
solution: 1) Run 'sudo /usr/libexec/mdmclient QueryInstalledApps > InstalledApps.txt' on device. 2) Compare installed apps list with Intune's included apps. 3) Remove apps from Intune that don't appear in InstalledApps.txt. 4) Save and sync device.

symptom: macOS 11.2.x devices: apps can't be downloaded/installed (Install Pending indefinitely), shell scripts don't run, Intune management agent installation fails. Error 506 'Install request is a duplicate' in device console log.
rootCause: Known macOS Big Sur 11.2.x bug causing ASDErrorDomain Code=506 duplicate install request errors.
solution: Upgrade the device to macOS 11.3 or later version.

symptom: Microsoft 365 apps for macOS close without notification and restart unexpectedly (e.g., Teams closes during a call)
rootCause: Multiple deployments of Microsoft 365 apps assigned as Required to the device, or known issue with the M365 Apps for macOS suite assigned as Required.
solution: 1) If multiple Required deployments: assign only one deployment. 2) If M365 suite assigned as Required: change assignment type to 'Available for enrolled devices'.

symptom: Microsoft 365 apps on macOS close without notification, update, and restart unexpectedly (no MAU deadline notification shown)
rootCause: Multiple configuration profiles managing Microsoft AutoUpdate (MAU) settings are deployed, or a single profile contains too many per-app MAU settings causing conflict.
solution: Deploy only one configuration profile for MAU settings. Include only MAU settings that apply to all apps in the Microsoft 365 Apps for macOS suite.

symptom: Intune App Wrapping Tool for Android crashes with 'The application could not be wrapped' error and ExceptionWithContext: Unsigned short value out of range
rootCause: The LOB app reaches or nearly reaches the 64K method reference limit of DEX files, preventing the wrapping tool from adding Intune code.
solution: 1) Install latest App Wrapping Tool version. 2) Enable multidex for the Android app. 3) If persists, reduce classes in primary DEX file and enable ProGuard code shrinking.

symptom: Outlook for Android shows 'Your organization has removed its data associated with this app' error, requiring re-sign-in
rootCause: Outlook went offline for too long (signed out by APP policy), or Outlook cannot detect Company Portal after system update and loses expected access token.
solution: 1) Turn off battery optimization for Outlook and Company Portal. 2) Change app Launch from Automatic to Manage Manually (enable Auto-launch, Secondary launch, Run in background). 3) Enable unrestricted data usage if Data Saver is on. 4) Enable notifications for both apps.

symptom: Discovered apps list in Intune shows what appear to be personal apps on Android Enterprise work profile devices
rootCause: By design: these are system apps/services duplicated in the work profile by the device OEM, not actual personal apps. Intune cannot inventory personal profile apps.
solution: No action needed. Verify by checking Settings > Work Profile > Apps on the device. Work apps show a briefcase icon.

symptom: Cannot attach photos in work profile apps (Word, Excel, Outlook, OneDrive) on Android Enterprise enrolled devices - 'Unable to add attachment due to IO error'
rootCause: Android requires an additional file explorer application in the workspace to open photos for attachment.
solution: Approve and assign a File Explorer app (e.g., Google Files) from managed Google Play store to the device. Then use the app's Attach icon to attach photos.

symptom: Outlook for Android shows 'This action is not allowed by your organization' when creating new email after APP policy is applied
rootCause: User signed in with Exchange Server on-premises account directly. Only Microsoft Entra accounts are supported by Intune APP; Exchange direct on-premises accounts are not supported.
solution: Sign in with a Microsoft Entra account after configuring Exchange hybrid Modern Authentication.

symptom: 'Safari cannot open the page because the address is invalid' error when opening a web app on iOS device
rootCause: Web app policy has 'Require a managed browser to open this link' set to Yes, but Intune Managed Browser for iOS is not installed on the device.
solution: Either install the Intune Managed Browser on the iOS device, or set 'Require a managed browser to open this link' to No in the policy.

symptom: Error 0x87D1FDE8 displayed in Intune admin console after deploying Managed Browser policy with allowlist URLs or during app installation
rootCause: Known issue in Microsoft Intune with Managed Browser policy deployment. The error is cosmetic and does not affect actual functionality.
solution: The error resolves automatically after the device checks in again. No action needed - safe to ignore.

symptom: Only one Intune app configuration policy is applied to Microsoft Edge or Managed Browser when multiple configuration profiles targeting the same settings are assigned
rootCause: Intune does not support merging Edge or Managed Browser app configuration policies. When multiple policies with overlapping settings target the same user, only one is applied.
solution: Configure each setting in only a single app configuration policy per user/group. Use exclusion groups to ensure no user is targeted by multiple policies with the same settings.

symptom: Save application failed. You don't have enough permissions to update this application error when updating an app in Intune with a custom role
rootCause: The application's targeted groups are not included in Scope (Groups) of the custom Intune role assignment.
solution: Add the application's targeted groups to Scope (Groups) in the custom role assignment.

symptom: Microsoft Store for Business volume-purchased apps do not synchronize and do not appear in the Intune portal
rootCause: The apps use the encrypted app package format (EAppxBundle), which is not supported for Intune synchronization.
solution: Distribute the affected apps through the private store instead of relying on Intune sync.

symptom: Samsung Galaxy Note 4 users not prompted for fingerprint authentication in Intune APP-managed apps despite 'Allow fingerprint instead of PIN' set to Yes
rootCause: Samsung Galaxy Note 4 uses a proprietary fingerprint management API not supported by Intune
solution: Use an alternate device that supports a fingerprint management API supported by Intune

symptom: Cannot assign SCEP certificates to devices after certificate renewal - NDESPlugin.log stops at 'Sending request to certificate registration point', CAPI2 log shows 'certificate not within validity period'
rootCause: NDES policy module still uses the thumbprint from an expired client authentication certificate that was selected during initial NDES/Intune Certificate Connector installation
solution: On NDES server: find expired client auth cert in certlm.msc → renew with new key → reinstall Intune Certificate Connector to link to new cert → restart Intune Connector Service and World Wide Web Publishing Service

symptom: iOS SCEP enrollment fails - IIS logs show good GetCACerts (200) but no GetCACaps request generated, device console shows 'The Registration Authority response is invalid' and SecTrustEvaluate errors
rootCause: Trusted Root certificate profile in Intune uses a different cert than NDES server's actual root (commonly an intermediate cert uploaded instead of root), or Root/Intermediate CA uses unsupported signature algorithm (RSASSA-PSS or SHA-1)
solution: Reissue new Root and Intermediate certificates with supported signature algorithm (e.g. SHA256RSA), redeploy on NDES server, ensure NDES-related certs reference the new trusted certs

symptom: SCEP certificate profile shows Failed; IIS logs show HTTP 500 on /CertificateRegistrationSvc/Certificate/VerifyRequest; Event ID 5009 (SCEP app pool crash) and Event ID 1310 (error code 3007 compilation error)
rootCause: CertificateRegistrationSvc app pool account (e.g. Network Service) missing Write permission on C:\Windows\Temp folder
solution: Grant Write permission to Network Service on C:\Windows\Temp; ensure no policies remove it afterward. Note: only affects legacy certificate connector.

symptom: Devices cannot obtain SCEP certificates; CA shows error 0x80094800 CERTSRV_E_UNSUPPORTED_CERT_TYPE; NDES Application log Event ID 31 with 0x80004005
rootCause: NDES registry template name mismatch - using friendly name instead of actual template name in HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MSCEP (SignatureTemplate/EncryptionTemplate/GeneralPurposeTemplate)
solution: Correct the registry values under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MSCEP to match the actual certificate template name on the CA, then restart Intune Connector Service

symptom: SCEP certificate deployment fails to Windows 10 devices after CA certificate renewal; Event 32, 307 in DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostic-Provider; Event 30 in CAPI2 with 800B0109 (root not trusted)
rootCause: RA certificates on NDES server still reference old CA certificate after CA renewal, causing trust chain failure
solution: Reinstall both the NDES server role and Microsoft Intune Certificate Connector to reissue RA certificates. Does not affect Android/iOS.

symptom: NDES connector installation fails with error 0x80090014 CryptAcquireContext failed in SetupMSI.log; NDES certificate not installed
rootCause: NDES certificate template is not a v2 (or later) template; CryptAcquireContext fails with legacy v1 templates
solution: Create the NDES certificate as a v2 template (Windows Server 2008 R2 or 2012 R2 template). Add Client Authentication as Enhanced Key Usage.

symptom: Intune client connectivity issues: devices cannot access Intune infrastructure, receive updates/policies, or complete new enrollments
rootCause: Automatic root certificate update mechanism disabled and Baltimore CyberTrust root certificate not installed on client
solution: Install latest root certificates from Microsoft Update Catalog (search 'root update'); install only the latest cumulative package. Or re-enable automatic root certificate update mechanism.

symptom: SCEP certificate request fails during CRP verification phase; Android/iOS devices do not receive certificates; CRP logs show CryptographicException: m_safeCertContext is an invalid handle
rootCause: NDES connector registry keys under HKLM\Software\Microsoft\MicrosoftIntune\NDESConnector are missing (signing certificate references not created)
solution: Solution 1: Restart Intune Connector Service and verify registry keys are created. Solution 2: Reset MSCEP template to default IPSECIntermediateOffline, restart server, verify signing certs appear, then set custom template name back.

symptom: SCEP certificate reporting phase fails: no new files appear in Succeed folder under CertificateRequestStatus; files stuck in Processing folder
rootCause: Intune Connector Service not started on NDES server, or errors in Ndesconnector.svclog preventing certificate status upload
solution: Verify Intune Connector Service is started. Check Ndesconnector.svclog for errors. Review IIS logs for POST to /CertificateRegistrationSvc/Certificate/Notify returning 204.

symptom: PKCS certificate deployment fails with error 0x800706BA RPC server unavailable; trusted root cert on device but PFX cert missing
rootCause: Cause 1: PKCS profile has wrong CA FQDN/name. Cause 2: CA cert renewed but UseDefinedCACertInRequest not enabled.
solution: Cause 1: Fix Certification authority and name in PKCS profile. Cause 2: Run certutil -setreg ca\UseDefinedCACertInRequest 1 then restart Certificate Services.

symptom: PKCS certificate fails with 0x80094015 enrollment policy server cannot be located
rootCause: Connector host cannot locate certificate enrollment policy server
solution: Run Add-CertificateEnrollmentPolicyServer PowerShell cmdlet on connector host.

symptom: PKCS submission pending: Taken Under Submission; PFX request in CA Pending Requests folder; devices cannot acquire certificates
rootCause: CA Policy Module set to require admin to explicitly issue certificate
solution: Change CA Policy Module to Follow the settings in the certificate template or automatically issue.

symptom: PKCS certificate fails with 0x80070057 parameter is incorrect; connector configured but devices get no certs
rootCause: PKCS profile misconfigured: wrong CA name or SAN for email but user has no email (null SAN)
solution: Verify PKCS profile CA name, user group assignment, and that users have valid email addresses.

symptom: PKCS certificate denied by Policy Module; device gets trusted root but not PFX
rootCause: Computer Account of connector server lacks Read and Enroll permissions on certificate template
solution: Add connector server Computer Account to cert template Security tab with Read + Enroll permissions, then re-issue template.

symptom: PKCS certificate profile stuck as Pending in Intune admin center; no obvious errors in logs
rootCause: Cause 1: PfxRequest files stuck in Failed/Processing folders. Cause 2: Wrong CA or root cert associated with PKCS profile.
solution: Check %programfiles%\Microsoft Intune\PfxRequest folders for errors. Verify trusted cert profile and PKCS profile reference correct CA.

symptom: PKCS fails with -2146875374 CERTSRV_E_SUBJECT_EMAIL_REQUIRED: Email name unavailable cannot be added to Subject
rootCause: Supply in the request option not enabled on certificate template Subject Name tab
solution: Open cert template Properties > Subject Name tab > select Supply in the request > re-issue template.

symptom: Intune Certificate Connector Setup Wizard ends prematurely; SetupMSI.log shows Error 0x80070002 Site not found and ConfigureIISLimits error 1168
rootCause: Default Web Site name in IIS Manager was renamed from its default value
solution: Open IIS Manager, find site with ID=1, rename back to Default Web Site, restart NDESConnectorSetup.exe.

symptom: NDES Connector UI sign-in error 0x80004003 unexpected error; svclog shows NDES Connector certificate could not be found
rootCause: Sign-in account lacks valid Intune license
solution: Assign valid Intune license to sign-in account (must be Intune Service Admin or Global Admin).

symptom: Intune Connector for Active Directory installed but not visible in Intune portal; ODJ event log shows Failed to get value for Key OdjServiceBaseUrl
rootCause: Proxy server blocks connector communication with Intune service; proxy not configured in ODJConnectorSvc.exe.config
solution: Add proxy config to ODJConnectorSvc.exe.config with system.net/defaultProxy element, then restart ODJConnector Service.

symptom: Intune Connector for AD sign-in unexpected error: An error occurred while processing your request
rootCause: Sign-in account not assigned Intune or Microsoft Office license
solution: Assign Intune license to the user account.

symptom: Co-management: Hybrid Azure AD join auto MDM enroll fails with error 0x8018002a. AADSTS50076 requiring MFA.
rootCause: MFA is Enforced (not just Enabled), preventing ConfigMgr client agent from enrolling.
solution: Set MFA to Enabled but not Enforced, or temporarily disable via Trusted IPs.

symptom: Co-management: Device sync fails after auto-enrollment. Error 0xcaa2000c interaction_required.
rootCause: MFA or CA policies requiring MFA applied to all cloud apps block user token.
solution: Disable per-user MFA, use Trusted IPs, or exclude Intune app from CA policies requiring MFA.

symptom: Co-management: Enroll fails with 0x800706D9 or 0x80180023. dmwappushservice missing.
rootCause: dmwappushservice service is missing from the device.
solution: Export dmwappushservice reg key from working device, merge on affected, restart twice with AD cleanup.

symptom: Co-management: Hybrid AAD join fails 0x801c03f2. Public key user certificate not found.
rootCause: UserCertificate attribute missing or not synced to Azure AD.
solution: dsregcmd /leave, delete MS-Organization-Access cert, restart, verify cert on AD object, delta sync.

symptom: Device registration fails 0x80280036. TPM FIPS mode.
rootCause: TPM has FIPS mode enabled, not supported for Azure device registration.
solution: Disable FIPS mode on TPM.

symptom: Hybrid AAD join fails 0x80090016 Keyset does not exist.
rootCause: Windows is not TPM owner. TPM init failed.
solution: Clear TPM via Windows Security. Disable BitLocker first. Restart.

symptom: Co-management bootstrap: Azure AD users not in ConfigMgr DB. Error 0x87d00231.
rootCause: API permissions or Azure AD user Discovery not configured.
solution: Configure API permissions and Azure AD user Discovery in ConfigMgr.

symptom: CMG connection point shows disconnected.
rootCause: Permissions issue: remote site system cannot access primary site inboxes.
solution: Add remote site system machine account to Local Admins on primary site.

symptom: Clients cannot locate MP via CMG, error 403 CMGConnector_Clientcertificaterequired.
rootCause: CRL validation failure for client certificate. Revocation server offline.
solution: Ensure CRL distribution points accessible or temporarily disable CRL checking for CMG.

symptom: ConfigMgr agent unhealthy in Intune, last check-in 2/1/1900.
rootCause: Compliance policies workload managed by ConfigMgr not Intune.
solution: Switch compliance workload to Intune or Pilot Intune.

symptom: Client apps workload not visible in co-management Properties.
rootCause: Pre-release feature not enabled.
solution: Enable pre-release feature in ConfigMgr: Administration > Updates and Servicing > Features.

symptom: Settings not reverted after unassigning Intune policies (no tattoo removal).
rootCause: Tattoo removal not supported when Device Configuration workload is ConfigMgr.
solution: Set Device Configuration workload to Intune, refresh policy on device.

symptom: Email profile: Users repeatedly prompted for password.
rootCause: Certificate profiles not assigned to same group type as email profile.
solution: Assign all cert profiles to same group type (user or device) consistently.

symptom: iOS user-created email profile blocks Intune profile deployment. Not compliant.
rootCause: Duplicate email profile detected based on hostname and email address.
solution: Remove manually created email profile before/after enrollment.

symptom: Samsung KNOX: EAS email profile error 0x87D1FDE8 remediation failed.
rootCause: Samsung Notes sync option selected but no longer supported.
solution: Deselect Samsung Notes sync option. Wait up to 24h.

symptom: Users cannot send images from auto-configured email accounts.
rootCause: Allow e-mail from third-party applications not enabled.
solution: Enable the setting in email profile Properties > Settings.

symptom: Password expiration setting not working. User not prompted after configured days.
rootCause: Editing the policy resets the timer even if the expiration value was not changed.
solution: Avoid editing policy unnecessarily. Expiry = N days after last modified date.

symptom: Deploying device configuration profile to iOS: error 2016341112 iOS device is currently busy. Profile not applied when device is locked.
rootCause: iOS device busy (locked/installing) sends NotNow status via MDM protocol.
solution: Unlock the iOS device. Profile applied automatically once unlocked.

symptom: Android Enterprise fully managed: password settings deployment fails when profile assigned after enrollment.
rootCause: Profile assigned after enrollment does not prompt user during setup.
solution: Assign device restrictions profile with password settings before enrollment.

symptom: Custom VPN profile (OMA-URI) reports 2016281112 Remediation Failed in Intune but VPN works on Win10.
rootCause: Win10 response XML differs from policy XML; Intune interprets mismatch as failure. Known issue up to Win10 1809.
solution: Ignore error or use standard VPN profile instead of custom OMA-URI.

symptom: Enterprise Mode site list not deployed to co-managed Win10 devices via Intune profile.
rootCause: ConfigMgr creates MicrosoftEdge EnterpriseMode SiteList registry that takes precedence over Intune.
solution: Set AllowConfigureMicrosoftEdge=0 under HKLM Policies Microsoft CCM; delete conflicting SiteList.

symptom: FRP emails not enforced as expected on Android Enterprise after factory reset.
rootCause: FRP varies by enrollment type/reset method. COPE: Settings reset enforced; COBO/COSU: only Recovery. Intune wipe does not preserve FRP.
solution: Block Factory reset in restrictions; use Recovery mode for FRP enforcement.

symptom: iOS email profile: no password prompt after password change. No email sent/received.
rootCause: EAS basic authentication limitation does not trigger re-auth prompt.
solution: Enable OAuth in email profile. Ensure email service supports OAuth first.

symptom: iOS Personal Hotspot restriction policy does not block on iOS 12.1 or earlier.
rootCause: Personal Hotspot restriction only enforced starting iOS 12.2.
solution: Upgrade to iOS 12.2 or later.

symptom: Users cannot log on to Entra-joined Windows 10/11 with multi-app kiosk profile assigned. Sign-in fails immediately before user profile loads. Event 31000 AssignedAccess error, 0xCAA2000C interaction_required.
rootCause: Conditional access policies requiring user interaction (MFA or TOU) conflict with multi-app kiosk mode which does not support interactive auth prompts.
solution: Exclude kiosk users from conditional access policies that require user interaction (MFA, TOU). Disable MFA for kiosk accounts.

symptom: Feature updates never offered on Intune-managed Windows 10 v1709+. Servicing/definition updates work. WindowsUpdate.log shows error 80070426 ERROR_SERVICE_NOT_ACTIVE for DCAT Flighting service.
rootCause: Microsoft Account sign-in assistant (MSA/wlidsvc) service disabled via Intune device restriction. DCAT Flighting service depends on MSA for global device ID.
solution: In Intune admin center > Device configuration > Profiles > Device restrictions > Cloud and Storage, set Microsoft Account sign-in assistant to Not configured.

symptom: Skype for Business on Android Enterprise does not save SIP address and password after app close or device restart. Users must re-enter credentials every time.
rootCause: Intune device restriction Add and Remove accounts set to Block prevents credential persistence on Android Enterprise work profile.
solution: In Device configuration > Profiles > Device restrictions > Work profile settings, change Add and Remove accounts to Not Configured.

symptom: Company Portal shows "We cannot update enrollment now. Try again later" notification when enrolling devices in Intune.
rootCause: Outdated version of the Company Portal app on the device.
solution: Update Company Portal to the latest version from the respective app store (Microsoft Store / App Store / Play Store).

symptom: Devices continue trying to enroll in Intune after subscription canceled and different MDM provider selected.
rootCause: CNAME DNS records (enterpriseenrollment/enterpriseregistration) still point to manage.microsoft.com / EnterpriseRegistration.windows.net, redirecting enrollment to Intune.
solution: Delete old CNAME records from domain registrar pointing to manage.microsoft.com and EnterpriseRegistration.windows.net. Set new CNAMEs per new MDM provider.

symptom: Android device cannot sign in to Company Portal: missing required certificate. ADFS intermediate cert not in SSL hello or user cert not trusted by Android 7.0+.
rootCause: ADFS server does not include intermediate certificates in SSL Server hello response; or ADFS uses user certificate (not publicly trusted CA) which Android 7.0+ ignores from user cert store.
solution: Sol 1: User installs missing cert. Sol 2: Import intermediate certs into ADFS Personal cert store, restart ADFS. Sol 3: Use cert chaining to publicly trusted root CA on ADFS.

symptom: Android Enterprise work profile device ownership auto-changed from Personal to Corporate after enrollment.
rootCause: Corporate device identifier (IMEI/serial) added for the device in Intune Admin Center.
solution: Delete the corporate identifier, then change device ownership back to Personal.

symptom: Microsoft Edge on Android 13 ignores PAC setting in per-app VPN profile. Edge does not consume proxy after VPN established.
rootCause: Android 13 behavior change causes Edge to ignore PAC when VPN established before Edge starts.
solution: Disconnect/reconnect VPN while Edge running. For Always-on VPN use airplane mode toggle. Alt: Postpone Android 13 upgrade.

symptom: iOS/iPadOS devices not checking in with Intune: Management State Unhealthy, cannot receive policies/apps/commands, users lose Conditional Access.
rootCause: Device cannot successfully sync with Intune; may not be properly enrolled or lost contact.
solution: Open Company Portal app - auto-detects lost contact and syncs. If Unable to sync, tap Set up to re-enroll via Company Access Setup flow.

symptom: iOS/iPadOS device stuck on enrollment screen 10+ min: Awaiting final configuration or Guided Access app unavailable.
rootCause: VPP token issue (expired, out of licenses, used by another service/tenant, deleted) with Single App Mode. Or Apple services outage.
solution: Check VPP token in Intune. Fix token issues. Filter blocked devices. Remotely wipe. Users restart enrollment.

symptom: iOS/iPadOS Profile Installation Failed: Network Error, Connection to server (corporate restriction, stale enrollment, another MDM, missing license), or MDM payload mismatch.
rootCause: Multiple: iOS corruption; device type restriction; previous enrollment not cleaned; existing MDM; missing license; existing management profile.
solution: Network error: backup, recovery restore, re-enroll. Connection: check restrictions, re-enroll via Safari, remove MDM, verify license. Payload mismatch: remove mgmt profile from Settings.

symptom: iOS device enrolled in Apple ADE does not start Intune enrollment process when turned on
rootCause: Enrollment profile created before ADE token upload to Intune, or no MDM profile assigned to device
solution: Ensure enrollment profile exists and is assigned; edit profile to update modification time; sync ADE token (Devices > iOS > Enrollment Program Tokens > Sync); then power on device

symptom: Apple ADE enrollment fails with XPC_TYPE_ERROR Connection invalid error in mobileassetd logs
rootCause: Network connection problem between device and Apple ADE service (cannot reach mesu.apple.com)
solution: Fix network connectivity or use a different network to enroll; if persists contact Apple support

symptom: macOS virtual machine enrollment fails with error 'It looks like you're using a virtual machine' or 'We're having trouble getting your device managed'
rootCause: VM not fully configured (missing serial/hardware model), enrollment restrictions block personal devices, or device already assigned to another user in Intune
solution: For VMs: fully configure serial number and hardware model; for personal device restrictions: manually add serial number as corporate identifier; for stale records: delete old device record from Intune admin center

symptom: macOS enrollment fails with keychain error -25244 (errSecInvalidOwnerEdit); Company Portal logs show 'failed to delete workplace join item' from keychain
rootCause: Stale or corrupted keychain entries related to Intune enrollment (workplace join items) in macOS keychain
solution: Open Keychain Access as local admin; delete all 'workplace' keys; delete specific Microsoft/CompanyPortal/enterpriseregistration keys; delete workplace join thumbprint/registrationKey/discoveryHint entries; delete MS-Organization-Access certificates and SC_Online_Issuing certs from System keychain; uninstall and reinstall Company Portal; re-enroll

symptom: Importing Windows Autopilot device CSV file fails with error 806 (ZtdDeviceAlreadyAssigned) or error 808 (ZtdDeviceAssignedToOtherTenant)
rootCause: Device already registered in the tenant (806) or assigned to another tenant (808); stale device record exists in Intune
solution: For 806: delete existing device record from Intune admin center (Devices > Windows > Enrollment > Autopilot Devices > Delete); for 808: contact Microsoft Support with device CSV, proof of ownership (bill of sale/invoice), and diagnostic logs (mdmdiagnosticstool.exe -area Autopilot -cab)

symptom: Cannot delete Windows Autopilot deployment profile in Intune; error 'The profile is assigned to groups. You must unassign all groups before you can delete it'
rootCause: Profile still assigned to groups in Microsoft Entra ID, or assigned group was deleted from Entra ID before being removed from profile assignments
solution: If group exists: remove all groups from Included groups in profile Assignments then delete; if group deleted: use Graph Explorer to find AutopilotProfileID and GroupID, then DELETE assignment via Graph API (DELETE .../assignments/{profileId}_{groupId}), then delete profile

symptom: Windows enrollment fails with error 0x8007064c The machine is already enrolled. Device was previously enrolled or cloned from an enrolled image; old Intune certificate and registry keys remain.
rootCause: Previous enrollment certificate (Sc_Online_Issuing) and registry key HKLM SOFTWARE Microsoft OnlineManagement persist from prior enrollment or cloned image.
solution: Delete the Intune cert issued by Sc_Online_Issuing from Local Computer Personal Certificates. Delete registry key HKLM SOFTWARE Microsoft OnlineManagement and HKCR Installer Products 6985F0077D3EEB44AB6849B5D7913E95 if present. Re-enroll.

symptom: Windows enrollment fails with error 8018000a The device is already enrolled. Another user has already enrolled or joined the device to Entra ID.
rootCause: A different user account has already enrolled the device in Intune or joined it to Microsoft Entra ID.
solution: Sign in with the other account that enrolled/joined the device. Go to Settings > Accounts > Work Access and remove the work or school connection. Sign back in with your account and retry enrollment.

symptom: Enrollment fails: Looks like the MDM Terms of Use endpoint is not correctly configured. Error connecting to MDM terms of use URL.
rootCause: User lacks valid Intune or Office 365 license, or MDM terms of use URL in Entra ID is blank/incorrect. Common when both MDM for M365 and Intune coexist on tenant.
solution: Assign valid Intune/M365 license to user. In Azure portal > Entra ID > Mobility (MDM and MAM) > Microsoft Intune, select Restore default MDM URLs and verify MDM terms of use URL is https://portal.manage.microsoft.com/TermsofUse.aspx. Save.

symptom: Entra join fails with error 80180026 on Windows 10 when MDM automatic enrollment is enabled and Intune PC software client (agent) is installed simultaneously.
rootCause: Conflict between Intune PC software client agent and MDM automatic enrollment. Both cannot coexist on the same device.
solution: Either disable MDM automatic enrollment in Azure (Entra ID > Mobility > Microsoft Intune > MDM User scope = None) OR uninstall the Intune PC software client agent from the computer.

symptom: Enrollment error 0x80180014: Your organization does not support this version of Windows. Windows MDM enrollment is disabled in the tenant.
rootCause: Windows (MDM) platform is set to Block in Intune enrollment restrictions (device type restriction).
solution: In Intune admin center > Devices > Enrollment restrictions, select device type restriction > Properties > Edit Platform settings > set Windows (MDM) to Allow. Review + Save.

symptom: Auto MDM Enroll: Failed with error 0x8018002b via Group Policy. Event ID 76 logged in DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/Admin.
rootCause: UPN contains unverified or non-routable domain suffix (e.g., .local), or MDM user scope is set to None in Entra ID.
solution: For UPN issue: change user UPN suffix to a valid verified domain in AD Users and Computers, then force delta sync (Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Delta). For MDM scope: set MDM user scope to All or Some in Entra ID > Mobility > Microsoft Intune.

symptom: Autopilot device enrollment fails with HRESULT 0x80180022. Device is running Windows Home Edition.
rootCause: Windows Home Edition does not support Autopilot/MDM enrollment. Only Pro edition or higher is supported.
solution: Update the device to Windows Pro edition or higher.

symptom: Autopilot self-deploying mode fails with error 0x800705b4: Securing your hardware (Failed). Subsequent steps also fail.
rootCause: Device lacks physical TPM 2.0 chip (virtual TPMs or TPM 1.2 not supported for self-deploying mode), or Windows version is below required build.
solution: Ensure device has a physical TPM 2.0 chip. Verify Windows 10 build 1709+ (or 1809+ for hybrid Entra join). VMs with virtual TPMs are not supported for self-deploying mode.

symptom: Autopilot error 80070774 in Hybrid Entra join scenario. Device times out during initial sign-in screen. Domain controller cannot be found.
rootCause: Assign user feature is configured in the Autopilot profile for a Hybrid Entra join scenario, causing Entra join during initial sign-in and preventing on-prem domain join.
solution: Unassign user from the Autopilot device in Intune admin center. Or check ODJ Connector Service log for Event 30132 and delegate proper permissions to the OU where Autopilot devices are created.

symptom: Enrollment Status Page (ESP) times out before sign-in screen loads when tracking Microsoft Store for Business apps with Conditional Access policy requiring device compliance.
rootCause: Conflict between ESP tracking Store for Business apps and CA policy requiring compliance. Compliance cannot be determined before user logon.
solution: Target Intune compliance policies to devices (not users) so compliance can be determined before logon. Or use offline licensing for store apps.

symptom: Windows enrollment error 0x801c003 or 80180003: This user is not authorized to enroll.
rootCause: (1) User reached max device limit, (2) Device type blocked by restrictions, (3) Windows 10 Home, (4) Entra Users may join devices set to None.
solution: (1) Remove unused devices or increase limit (max 15). (2) Set Windows (MDM) to Allow. (3) Upgrade to Pro+. (4) Set Users may join devices to All in Entra Device Settings.

symptom: Windows OOBE Set up for work or school enrollment fails with error 80180014. Enrollment blocked during device setup.
rootCause: Device recognized as personal device during OOBE. Personal device enrollment blocked in Intune enrollment restrictions.
solution: In Intune admin center > Enrollment device platform restrictions > Windows restrictions, create restriction with Personally owned devices set to Allow. Assign to appropriate groups.

symptom: Windows 10 GP-based auto-enrollment fails with 0x80180002b. dsregcmd /status shows AzureAdPrt=NO and incorrect TenantId/AuthCodeUrl.
rootCause: Device previously joined to different Entra tenant without proper unjoin. Stale tenant info in registry CloudDomainJoin TenantInfo causes incorrect PRT.
solution: dsregcmd /leave, delete device in Entra, unjoin from AD, delete device from DC, rejoin AD, delta sync, verify AzureAdJoined/DomainJoined/AzureAdPrt=YES, gpupdate /force.

symptom: Windows enrollment via Company Portal fails: You do not have the right privileges. Non-admin account cannot enroll already-configured device.
rootCause: User is not local Administrator. Local admin required for enrolling already-configured Windows 10 device via Company Portal.
solution: Use Windows Autopilot or join brand-new device via OOBE (uses SYSTEM account, no local admin needed).

symptom: MDM enrollment error 0xcaa9001f for co-managed Hybrid Entra joined devices via ConfigMgr: Integrated Windows authentication supported only in federation flow.
rootCause: IWA attempted against non-federated Entra domain. Cloud Management Azure service not configured in ConfigMgr, or AD/Entra user Discovery not both enabled.
solution: Configure Azure Services for Cloud Management in ConfigMgr. Enable both AD User Discovery and Entra user Discovery.

symptom: Sysprep does not remove MDM enrollment (Intune) certs/IDs/settings from Windows 10 device. Cloned devices share same device IDs causing MDM failures.
rootCause: Sysprep generalization does not clean MDM enrollment data or Entra join info. Cloning enrolled device creates duplicate identities.
solution: Never clone Entra-joined/Intune-enrolled devices. Image in workgroup state using MDT/ConfigMgr OSD. Run sysprep /generalize before capture. Use Windows Autopilot for post-deployment.

symptom: Hybrid Entra joined Windows device shows Windows failed to apply the MDM Policy settings warning when running gpupdate /force
rootCause: Expected behavior - Auto MDM Enrollment GPO tries to enroll already enrolled device, error 0x8018000a
solution: Safely ignore the warning. Error 0x8018000a confirms device already enrolled.

symptom: Windows 10 device cannot restart after Intune remote Wipe action
rootCause: Wipe device and continue to wipe even if devices lose power option selected with Windows installation corruption
solution: Use bootable media to reinstall Windows 10. For BitLocker encrypted devices same resolution.

symptom: Wipe or Retire action shows as Pending indefinitely in Intune console
rootCause: Devices do not always report status back to Intune before reset/removal starts
solution: Verify action succeeded on device then manually delete device record from Intune.

symptom: Reset Passcode action greyed out on Android Device Admin enrolled device
rootCause: Google removed passcode reset from Device Admin API for Android 7.0+ as ransomware protection
solution: Platform limitation by Google. Consider migrating to Android Enterprise management.

symptom: Not Supported message when issuing passcode reset to Android 8.0+ personally owned work profile device
rootCause: Reset token not activated on device
solution: 1) Require work profile passcode in config policy 2) User sets passcode 3) User accepts secondary prompt to allow reset.

symptom: Disable Activation Lock code not visible in Hardware overview for iOS/iPadOS device
rootCause: Code expired and cleared from service or device not Supervised with Device Restriction Policy
solution: Check code via Graph Explorer GET /beta/deviceManagement/manageddevices(deviceId)?$select=activationLockBypassCode. Ensure device is Supervised.

symptom: Device UPN shows as None in Intune admin center under All Devices
rootCause: User deleted from Entra ID before enrolled device was deleted from Intune creating orphaned device
solution: Run RemoveIntuneDevice.ps1 script. Prerequisites: Entra ID recycle bin enabled, Graph permissions, MS Graph PowerShell module.

symptom: Help desk operators receive Initiating Factory reset failed error performing remote tasks in Intune
rootCause: Help Desk Operator role assigned to dynamic device groups instead of user groups
solution: Reassign Help Desk Operator role to user groups instead of dynamic device groups.

symptom: iTunes Encrypt local backup option automatically selected and cannot be deselected for Intune-managed iOS device
rootCause: Intune certificate profile causes iTunes to force backup encryption. Certificates flagged as Always nonmigratory by Apple design.
solution: Expected behavior by Apple security design. No workaround available.

symptom: RemoteWipe doWipe fails on Windows 10 with Event ID 400 The request is not supported (0x80070032)
rootCause: Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE) is disabled. RemoteWipe CSP requires WinRE.
solution: Run Reagentc /info as admin to check WinRE status. If Disabled troubleshoot and re-enable WinRE.

symptom: Windows 10 device cannot sync with Intune after enrollment (randomly 2 min to 2 days) manual sync fails no errors logged
rootCause: dmwappushservice (Device Management WAP Push) service is disabled often by a deployed PowerShell script
solution: Set dmwappushservice startup type to Automatic.

symptom: Exchange Connector configuration fails with error 0x0000001: The Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector cannot connect to the Microsoft Exchange server
rootCause: Internet proxy settings are misconfigured, preventing the Exchange Connector from reaching the Exchange server
solution: Configure proxy using Netsh winhttp set proxy with correct proxy server and bypass list for internal Exchange addresses

symptom: Exchange Connector configuration fails with error 0x000000b: CertEnroll::CX509PrivateKey::Create: The system cannot find the file specified. 0x80070002
rootCause: The account used to sign in to Intune is not a Global Administrator account
solution: Sign in to Intune with a Global Administrator account, or add the current account to the Global Admin group

symptom: Exchange Connector configuration fails with error 0x0000006: The Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector cannot connect to Microsoft Intune
rootCause: A proxy server is blocking traffic to the Intune service
solution: Either remove proxy settings or configure the proxy to allow communication to the Intune service endpoints

symptom: Exchange Connector service fails to start with Event 7000 or 7041: The service did not start because of a logon failure / WIEC service unable to log on
rootCause: The WIEC_USER service account does not have the Log on as a service user right in local policy, or Group Policy is overwriting it
solution: Assign Log on as a service right to WIEC_USER via Local Security Policy (secpol.msc > User Rights Assignment). For clustered environments, assign on all nodes

symptom: Jamf Pro shows Could not retrieve the access token for Microsoft Graph API when configuring Intune integration
rootCause: Required TCP ports for Jamf-Intune communication are blocked by firewall or proxy (ports 443, 2195, 2196, 5223 for devices; 80, 5223 for Jamf Pro)
solution: Unblock TCP ports: 443 (Intune), 2195/2196/5223 (macOS devices), 80/5223 (Jamf Pro)

symptom: Jamf console shows Unable to connect to Microsoft Intune and Unable to send inventory information; connection status shows Terminated in Intune admin center
rootCause: Jamf license has expired
solution: Contact Jamf Software to renew the license

symptom: iOS/iPadOS device appears compliant but user is blocked by Conditional Access; Company Portal reports noncompliant due to manually configured email profile
rootCause: An existing manually configured email profile on the iOS device blocks deployment of the Intune admin-created email profile, making device noncompliant
solution: User must remove the existing manual email profile from the device, then the Intune email profile can deploy successfully. Instruct users to remove existing profiles before enrollment

symptom: Android device appears encrypted but Company Portal marks it as noncompliant/not encrypted
rootCause: Some Android manufacturers encrypt using a default PIN instead of a user-set PIN. Intune views default-PIN encryption as insecure and marks device noncompliant
solution: User must set a new non-default startup PIN: tap Company Portal notification, confirm existing PIN, choose Require PIN to start device on Secure startup screen, then tap Check Compliance

symptom: BitLocker silent encryption fails with conflicting Group Policy settings error in BitLocker-API event log
rootCause: Conflicting TPM startup settings: configuring TPM startup PIN or startup key to Allowed while requiring silent encryption creates a policy conflict
solution: Set Compatible TPM startup PIN and Compatible TPM startup key to Blocked in BitLocker policy when silent encryption is required

symptom: BitLocker silent encryption fails: TPM is not available / A compatible Trusted Platform Module Security Device cannot be found on this computer
rootCause: TPM chip is missing, disabled in BIOS, or unhealthy. TPM is required for silent encryption
solution: Verify TPM via TPM.msc or Get-Tpm cmdlet. If disabled, enable in BIOS. For TPM 2.0, ensure UEFI BIOS mode. If unhealthy, clear/reset via TPM management console

symptom: BitLocker device shows error state in Intune encryption report despite being encrypted; encryption method mismatch between policy and device
rootCause: Device was previously encrypted with a different method (e.g., XTS-AES 256-bit vs 128-bit) than Intune policy specifies, via manual/MBAM/ConfigMgr before enrollment
solution: Decrypt the device manually or with PowerShell, then let Intune BitLocker policy re-encrypt with the correct method on next policy sync

symptom: BitLocker-encrypted Windows 10 device shows as Not Compliant in Intune even though BitLocker Drive Encryption is enabled and compliance policy requires device encryption
rootCause: BitLocker encryption has not finished yet. Based on disk size, number of files, and BitLocker settings, encryption can take a long time
solution: Wait for BitLocker encryption to complete. After encryption is finished, the device will show as Compliant

symptom: Windows 10 device with Secure Boot enabled shows as Not Compliant in Intune compliance policy that requires Secure Boot
rootCause: The Require Secure Boot setting is only supported on TPM 2.0 devices with UEFI firmware. Devices with TPM 1.2 or legacy BIOS will show Not Compliant even if Secure Boot is actually enabled
solution: Verify device has TPM 2.0 (run tpm.msc), UEFI BIOS mode (msinfo32), and PCR7 bound. If TPM is 1.2, contact manufacturer to update to TPM 2.0. Run Confirm-SecureBootUEFI and manage-bde to verify protectors

symptom: Windows 10 devices with Microsoft Defender Firewall enabled show incorrect compliance status (noncompliant) in Intune when Firewall setting is set to Require
rootCause: Known issue in certain Windows 10 versions (1809, 1803, 1709) causing incorrect firewall compliance reporting
solution: Install specific KB updates: KB4469342 (1809), KB4467682 (1803), KB4467681 (1709). Workaround: set grace period for noncompliance action or set Firewall to Not configured, then ask users to manually sync

symptom: Cannot access company resources on ADE (Apple Automated Device Enrollment) device enrolled in Intune with Conditional Access. Error: Your sign-in was successful but your admin requires your device to be managed
rootCause: Enrollment profile has Authentication method set to Setup Assistant and Company Portal app is not deployed to the ADE device. Company Portal is required for Conditional Access enforcement
solution: Solution 1: Change Authentication method to Company Portal in enrollment profile (Devices > iOS/iPad > Enrollment Program Tokens > Profile). Solution 2: Deploy Company Portal app to the device via app configuration policy

symptom: Error -2016281112 when deploying password policy (device restriction) in Intune for Android or Windows desktop devices
rootCause: Password policies via device restriction cannot be immediately enforced on users. If user does not change their password as required by the policy, the error persists. Domain accounts are not evaluated locally for EAS password policies
solution: Direct users to change their password (Android: accept notification; Windows: Ctrl+Alt+Del > Change Password). Recommend deploying device compliance policy instead of device restriction for password enforcement to block noncompliant devices at enrollment

symptom: Intune-licensed users are unexpectedly prompted to enroll in Intune when accessing Microsoft 365 resources, even though Conditional Access is not configured to enforce Intune MDM enrollment
rootCause: Basic Mobility and Security for Microsoft 365 security policies are still deployed to a group containing the affected users. Microsoft Entra authentication services check group membership for BMB policies and enforce Conditional Access regardless of user authority setting
solution: Remove affected users from groups assigned Basic Mobility and Security security policies, or remove the BMB security policy entirely if no longer needed

symptom: Intune Endpoint Protection engine unavailable, features disabled (real-time protection, download scanning, behavior monitoring, script scanning, Network Inspection System), or malware definitions out of date (14+ days)
rootCause: Endpoint protection engine corrupted/deleted, features disabled by admin via configuration profile or by end user, or device disconnected from Internet preventing definition updates
solution: Engine unavailable: force update or uninstall/reinstall Endpoint Protection Agent (auto-reinstalls at next sync). Features disabled: enable via Endpoint Protection settings or Device Restrictions profile. Definitions outdated: update via Microsoft Defender Antivirus settings. Scan overdue: run one-time scan or schedule recurring scans

symptom: Script Error 'Unable to get property Content of undefined or null reference' when configuring Microsoft Intune subscription in Configuration Manager setup wizard
rootCause: Login account has Delegated Administration permissions on multiple subscriptions. Multi-account console is not supported during Intune setup on Configuration Manager
solution: Use a Global Administrator account that does not have Delegated Administration permissions

symptom: Error AADSTS50011 'The reply url specified in the request does not match the reply urls configured for the application' when querying Intune Data Warehouse via PowerShell or custom application
rootCause: Default redirect URI for the application registration or PowerShell script has been removed
solution: For PowerShell: set redirectUri to 'urn:ietf:wg:oauth:2.0:oob'. For custom app: go to Azure portal > Entra ID > App registrations > find app by ID > Authentication > add redirect URI type 'Public client (mobile & desktop)' with value 'urn:ietf:wg:oauth:2.0:oob'

symptom: 403 Forbidden error when querying Intune objects in Graph Explorer under https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/deviceManagement namespace. Error mentions missing DeviceManagementConfiguration.Read.All or ReadWrite.All scopes
rootCause: Account used to access Graph Explorer lacks required Intune read/write permissions (DeviceManagementConfiguration.Read.All, DeviceManagementConfiguration.ReadWrite.All)
solution: In Graph Explorer, click 'modify your permissions' in error message, enable DeviceManagementConfiguration.Read.All and ReadWrite.All, select Modify Permissions, accept consent prompt. If still failing, delete Graph Explorer enterprise app in Entra ID > Enterprise Applications and re-consent

symptom: NAC (Network Access Control) partner integration returns HTTP 503 errors when querying Intune for device compliance state
rootCause: Broad unfiltered queries to Intune are throttled: after the first 60 minutes of unfiltered calls, Intune returns 503 for the next 3 hours (4-hour throttle window). Device-specific filtered calls are never throttled
solution: Limit broad unfiltered NAC queries to once every 4+ hours (within the first 60-minute window). Use device-specific calls (filtered by IMEI or Wi-Fi MAC) for individual device checks — these are unlimited and never return 503

symptom: Cannot load Help and support blade in Intune admin center. Browser shows 'login.microsoftonline.com refused to connect' error (Chrome/Firefox) or blank page (Safari/Edge)
rootCause: Browser is configured to block third-party cookies, preventing the Help and support blade iframe from loading
solution: Configure browser to allow third-party cookies: click the cookie-blocked icon in address bar, show cookies, allow each blocked site individually, then refresh the page

symptom: Intune Troubleshoot pane displays 'Account status You do not have enough permissions' banner for Help Desk Operator role members. No user or device data is shown
rootCause: The Help Desk Operator user is not licensed for Intune
solution: In Intune admin center > Users > All Users, select the affected user > Licenses tab > Assign an Intune license. Then advise the user to access the troubleshooting portal in a new browser session
